title: woodwardia radicans
intro: Woodwardia radicans, commonly known as the resurrection fern or tassel fern, is a type of epiphytic fern native to the southeastern United States and the Caribbean. It is known for its unique ability to dry out completely and then revive when exposed to water again. The medicinal properties of Woodwardia radicans include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which may be used to support respiratory health, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being.
